name: Stripe MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Stripe MCP server from the Stripe agent toolkit. Enables AI agents to interact with Stripe's payment APIs — creating payment intents, managing customers, subscriptions, invoices, products, prices, and refunds. Exposes core Stripe financial operations to LLM agents.
use_cases: Creating and managing Stripe payment intents from e-commerce agents
Managing customer subscriptions and billing from SaaS automation agents
Generating and sending invoices from financial workflow agents
Retrieving payment and revenue analytics from business intelligence agents
not_for: Fraud investigation workflows requiring human review
best_when: A SaaS or e-commerce developer wants AI agents to automate Stripe billing operations — managing subscriptions, handling refunds, generating invoices, and analyzing revenue data.
avoid_when: Agents operating autonomously on payment flows without human oversight. FINANCIAL RISK: always implement approval gates before creating charges or refunds.

auth.notes: Stripe restricted API keys recommended for agents. Use test mode keys during development. Restrict key permissions to minimum required operations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Stripe API free; transaction fees apply per payment processed
pricing.notes: MCP server is free open source. Stripe charges per transaction. Test mode is free with no charges.

performance.rate_limits: Stripe API: 100 read/write requests per second per key

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Agents can create real charges and refunds — always use test mode first
Use restricted API keys with minimal permissions for agent access
Webhook idempotency key handling required for reliable payment processing
Stripe test mode vs live mode: verify agents can't accidentally hit live endpoints
PCI scope: avoid storing raw card numbers; use Stripe Elements/PaymentIntents
agent_readiness.security_notes: FINANCIAL RISK: Real payment operations. Use restricted keys. Implement approval gates. PCI compliance required for card data. Test mode for development.

security_score_components.security_notes: Stripe is PCI-DSS compliant. Restricted keys essential. Financial operations require human oversight gates. HTTPS enforced.
